back_translation: It came to pass one day, while He was praying in a certain place — when He had finished praying, one of His disciples said to Him, 'Lord, please teach us to pray, as John taught his disciples.'
He said to them, 'When you pray, say: "Father, may Your name be held in reverent worship. May Your kingdom come.'
Give our daily food to us every day.
Forgive our sins, for we also forgive everyone who is indebted to us. And do not lead us into temptation.'
Then He said to them, 'Suppose one of you has a friend, and goes to that friend at midnight and says to him, "Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,'
because my friend has traveled to me, and I have nothing to serve him to eat.'

And suppose the person inside answers from within, 'Do not disturb me. The door has already been shut and locked. My children are also lying on the bed with me. I cannot get up and fetch anything to give you.'
I tell you, even though he will not get up to give because of friendship, yet because of the shamelessness of the one persistently asking, he will get up and give him as much as he needs.
So I tell you: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks, it will be opened.
What father among you, if his son asks for a fish, will give him a snake instead of a fish?
Or if he asks for an egg, will he give him a scorpion?
Therefore, if you who are sinful still know how to give good things to your children, how much more will the Father who dwells in heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him!'
Later on, He was driving out a demon that was causing muteness. When the demon had gone out, that mute man was able to speak. The crowds were all amazed.
But some of them said, 'This one drives out demons by the power of Beelzebul, who is the ruler of the demons.'
And others were testing Him by demanding a sign from heaven.
But He knew their thoughts, and said to them, 'Any kingdom divided against itself will collapse; and a household divided against a household will also fall.
And if Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom stand firm? For you say that I drive out demons by Beelzebul.
And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, then by whom do your descendants drive out demons? For this reason, they will be your judges.
But if I drive out demons by the finger of God's hand, then the kingdom of God has already come upon you.
When a strong man who is fully armed guards his own house, his possessions are safe.
But when one who is stronger than he attacks and defeats him, that one seizes the full armor in which he had trusted, and distributes the spoils that were taken.
The one who is not on my side is against me, and the one who does not gather with me scatters.
When an unclean spirit has gone out of a person, it wanders through arid places seeking a resting-place but does not find one. Then it says, 'I will return to my house from which I came out.'
Upon arriving, it finds that the house has been swept and put in order.
Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more wicked than itself. They enter and dwell there, and the final state of that person becomes worse than the beginning.'
While He was speaking these words, a certain woman in the crowd raised her voice and called out to Him, 'Blessed is the womb that bore You, and the breasts that gave You milk to drink.'
But He replied, 'Rather, blessed are those who hear and keep the word of God.'
As the crowds were increasing in number, He began to say, 'This generation is a wicked generation. They seek a sign, but no sign will be given to them except the sign of Jonah.
For as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Son of Man will also be a sign to this generation.

The queen of the south will rise up on the day of judgment together with this generation and will condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, One greater than Solomon is here.

The people of Nineveh will rise up on the day of judgment together with this generation and will condemn them, because the people of Nineveh repented when they heard the proclamation of Jonah, and behold, One greater than Jonah is here.
No one lights a lamp and places it in a hidden place or under a basket, but rather sets it on a lampstand, so that those who come in may see the light.
Your eye is the lamp of the body. When your eye is good, your whole body will be full of light. But when your eye is not good, your body will be full of darkness.
Therefore, be careful that the light which is in you is not darkness.
Therefore, if your whole body is full of light, with no part remaining dark, the body will be wholly full of light, as when a lamp shines with brilliant radiance upon you.'
When He had finished speaking, a Pharisee invited Him to come and eat a meal with him. So He went in and reclined at the table.
But that Pharisee was surprised to see that He did not wash before eating.
The Lord then said to him, 'Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and dish, but the inside of you is full of greed and wickedness.
Fools, did not He who made the outside also make the inside?
But give the things that are inside as alms, and behold, all things will be clean for you.

But woe to you Pharisees, for you give tithes of mint, rue, and every kind of herb, yet disregard justice and the love of God. These things should already have been practiced, without neglecting the others as well.
Woe to you Pharisees, for you love the important seats in the synagogues, and the greetings in the marketplaces.
Woe to you, for you are like graves that have no marker, which people walk across without realizing.'
Then one of the experts in the law said to Him, 'Teacher, when you say these things, you also insult us.'
He said, 'Woe to you experts in the law as well, for you place burdens too heavy to carry upon people, but you yourselves do not touch those burdens even with a single fingertip.
Woe to you, for you build tombs for the prophets, even though your ancestors are the ones who killed those prophets.
Therefore, you are witnesses and approve of the deeds of your ancestors, for they killed the prophets, and you build tombs for them.
Because of this, the wisdom of God said, 'I will send prophets and apostles to them, and they will kill and persecute some of those I send.'
So that this generation may be required to give account for the blood of all the prophets that has been shed from the beginning of the world,
from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who was killed between the altar and the house of God. I tell you, yes, this generation will be required to be held responsible for this matter.
Woe to you experts in the law, for you have taken away the key of knowledge. You yourselves have not entered in, and you have also hindered those who were about to enter.'
When He had gone out from there, the scribes and Pharisees began to oppose Him fiercely and to cross-examine Him about many things,
waiting to ambush Him, so as to catch something wrong in the words that came out of His mouth.
